audit_items.proof_trace: OpenSSL documents X509_STORE_CTX_get_current_cert() as returning NULL when no certificate is relevant to the verification error; the code at L1536 does not check for NULL before passing cert to X509_get_subject_name(cert) at L1544 and X509_get_issuer_name(cert) at L1545, both of which dereference the pointer internally. A secondary unchecked NULL dereference exists in BIO_meth_new (L140-144, pre-1.1 only) where LDAP_MALLOC's result is used immediately without a NULL guard.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker acting as a TLS peer (server or client) presents a certificate chain that triggers an OpenSSL verification error condition where X509_STORE_CTX_get_current_cert() returns NULL; the resulting call to X509_get_subject_name(NULL) in tlso_verify_cb crashes the OpenLDAP process, causing denial of service.
